symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic boring epigastric pain radiating to the back, eased by leaning forward
symptoms.character: deep, boring, epigastric, radiating through to the back; relieved by sitting forward

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Boring epigastric-to-back pain relieved by leaning forward is the classic chronic pancreatitis pain pattern.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe pain flare refractory to maximal oral opioid after a fatty meal
symptoms.onset: over the last 48 hours, after a large fatty meal

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A fatty-meal-triggered flare refractory to the patient's maximal oral opioid is the defining feature of an acute-on-chronic pancreatitis crisis.
A sudden severe change refractory to usual analgesia should prompt a search for a structural complication.

symptoms.symptom_name: Significant unintentional weight loss with preserved appetite

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Significant unintentional weight loss with epigastric pain is a cardinal pancreatic-cancer alarm feature mandating urgent imaging to exclude malignancy.
Fat malabsorption contributes to weight loss, but malabsorption must not be assumed to fully explain it without excluding malignancy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Steatorrhoea (bulky, pale, greasy, floating, foul-smelling stools)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Steatorrhoea is the hallmark of pancreatic exocrine failure and confirms advanced chronic pancreatitis.
Pancreatogenic diabetes from islet destruction is part of the chronic pancreatitis tetrad and adds glycaemic-decompensation risk during acute illness.

symptoms.symptom_name: History of heavy alcohol use, now abstinent 2 years

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic heavy alcohol use is the leading cause of chronic pancreatitis in adults and explains the underlying structural disease.
Absence of bleeding lowers the immediate haemorrhage risk but must still be safety-netted as it can develop.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever/rigors and no jaundice (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever/rigors/jaundice lowers the current probability of biliary sepsis but does not exclude an obstructing complication developing.

symptoms.symptom_name: Steady boring pain (not sudden tearing) with fluids still tolerated (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A steady pattern with tolerated fluids argues against a catastrophic perforation or complete obstruction now, supporting urgent ED rather than emergency-ambulance triage — but cannot exclude either remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cachectic, malnourished appearance and low body weight (55 kg)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cachexia reflects advanced exocrine failure and raises concern for malignancy; the clinician's overall impression is a sealed in-person observation, though the patient can self-report low weight.

symptoms.symptom_name: Epigastric tenderness on deep palpation without guarding, rebound, rigidity or a palpable mass; active bowel sounds; no clinical jaundice

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Deep epigastric tenderness without peritonism fits pancreatitis rather than perforation — a sealed clinician examination the AI Doctor cannot perform, which is precisely why urgent in-person assessment is required.
Absence of guarding/rebound/rigidity on examination argues against a perforation, but only an in-person examination can establish this.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia (HR ~100) and blood pressure ~140/90 measured by clinician

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild tachycardia and mild hypertension are non-specific and consistent with severe pain; a sealed clinician measurement not available to the AI Doctor via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Serum lipase (markedly elevated in an acute exacerbation)

investigation_details.expected_result: Elevation supports an acute pancreatic inflammatory component; may be less dramatically raised in burnt-out chronic disease
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: An acute inflammatory flare or complication is not objectively confirmed and severity cannot be stratified.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A raised lipase supports acute inflammation but is neither sensitive nor specific in advanced chronic disease — a hospital blood test never available in real time via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: CT abdomen (pancreatic calcification; pseudocyst, obstruction or mass characterisation)

investigation_details.expected_result: Calcified, atrophic pancreas confirming chronic pancreatitis; identifies or excludes a pseudocyst, biliary/duodenal obstruction, or a pancreatic mass
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: An acute complication or a pancreatic malignancy is neither confirmed nor excluded — the central unresolved question of the case.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cross-sectional imaging is the definitive test to characterise the pancreas and exclude a complication or malignancy — never available via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Established alcohol-related chronic pancreatitis with exocrine and endocrine failure
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Chronic boring epigastric-to-back pain, steatorrhoea, brittle diabetes and a heavy-alcohol aetiology (with pancreatic calcification on prior imaging) together confirm advanced chronic pancreatitis — but confirm only the chronic disease, not the acute complication or malignancy.

A refractory change in a chronic pain pattern plus significant unintentional weight loss and cachexia in a chronic pancreatitis patient mandates urgent pancreatic imaging to exclude cancer, regardless of the reassuring chronic label.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Characterises the pancreas and is the key test to exclude an acute complication (pseudocyst, biliary/duodenal obstruction) and a pancreatic mass — the central unresolved questions.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum lipase, LFTs, FBC, CRP, lipids, calcium and lactate
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms/stratifies an acute inflammatory component, screens for biliary obstruction and sepsis, and assesses the cachectic, brittle-diabetic patient's metabolic state.
Brittle pancreatogenic diabetes with a severe intercurrent illness and altered intake risks DKA or hypoglycaemia — glucose and ketones must be checked in-person.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CA 19-9 and dedicated pancreatic-protocol imaging (MRI/EUS) after the acute episode
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To pursue exclusion of pancreatic malignancy prompted by the weight loss, once the acute crisis is stabilised.
Confirms exocrine pancreatic insufficiency and guides enzyme replacement — a routine outpatient test, not part of the acute assessment.
